description_long: This is a stone or concrete bridge featuring three prominent arches that span its length, supported by four robust rectangular piers. The bridge has a solid parapet running along the top, with subtle decorative elements at the corners and above each pier, giving it a sense of classical symmetry. The surface appears slightly weathered, suggesting durability and age, while the overall structure is low and wide, suitable for pedestrian or light vehicular traffic. The arches are evenly spaced and provide open passageways beneath, typical of traditional masonry bridges. The parapet is solid and blocky, lacking any open railings or intricate ornamentation. The color palette is dominated by dark gray tones, enhancing its utilitarian and timeless appearance.
description: A dark gray, stone or concrete bridge with three large arches and a solid parapet, supported by four rectangular piers. The design is classical and robust, with subtle decorative blocks at the corners and above each pier.
description_view_0: End-on view showing the solid, blocky parapet and the rectangular profile of the supporting pier.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the three large arches, the solid parapet, and the full length of the bridge.
description_view_2: Opposite end-on view, similar to view 0, showing the rectangular pier and parapet from the other side.
description_view_3: Alternate side view, mirroring view 1, with the three arches and solid parapet visible.
attribute_railing_design: solid concrete parapet with blocky decorative elements at corners and above piers
